matches.match_rationale: Accreditor standards (ACSEE, ACPE, AACN) are dense documents with overlapping but distinct language. A RAG system grounded in the actual standards text lets compliance staff query 'how does ACPE define clinical hours?' and receive a cited, accurate answer — directly preventing the misinterpretation that triggers corrective action. Unlike keyword search, it handles paraphrased or ambiguous queries against multi-accreditor corpora simultaneously.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance Q&A assistant that indexes all active accreditor handbooks and school policy documents, answering requirement questions with source citations and flagging when multiple accreditors define the same concept differently.

matches.match_rationale: The core problem is that overlapping requirements from different accreditors are stored in separate documents with no explicit cross-reference. Knowledge extraction can systematically pull requirement triples (accreditor → standard → specific rule) and build a structured cross-accreditor map, making conflicts and gaps machine-readable rather than dependent on staff memory. This directly surfaces the 'clinical hours counted differently by ACSEE vs school policy' class of conflict.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated pipeline that ingests accreditor standards PDFs and outputs a structured requirements graph, enabling side-by-side comparison of how each body defines shared concepts like clinical hours, credit loads, or faculty qualifications.

matches.match_rationale: Once requirements are structured, each curriculum element or policy statement can be classified as compliant/non-compliant against each accreditor's standard. This turns a manual audit-prep process into an automated screening pass that flags misalignments before an external auditor does. The multi-label nature (one curriculum element, multiple accreditors) is well within standard NLP classification capability.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A curriculum compliance screener that takes course descriptions or policy text as input and outputs a per-accreditor compliance label with a confidence score, surfacing misalignments for human review before submission cycles.

matches.match_rationale: The institution can be scored on the probability of a compliance finding per program-accreditor pair based on historical corrective action data, curriculum change frequency, and standards update recency. This converts reactive discovery (auditor finds non-compliance) into proactive prioritization of which programs to review first. The 'months of corrective action' consequence makes the adverse outcome concrete enough to build a meaningful risk model.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A program-level compliance risk dashboard that scores each degree program against its accreditor on a rolling basis, factoring in curriculum changes, accreditor standard updates, and prior audit findings — enabling the compliance team to triage their limited review time.

matches.match_rationale: When non-compliance is identified, drafting corrective action plans (CAPs) for each accreditor is a high-effort, format-specific writing task. Each accreditor (ACSEE, ACPE, AACN) has distinct CAP templates and preferred language. AI text generation can draft CAPs from structured finding inputs, reducing the months-consuming documentation burden and ensuring format compliance with each body's submission requirements.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A CAP drafting assistant that takes a structured compliance gap description as input and generates a formatted corrective action plan tailored to the specific accreditor's template, tone, and required attestation language.

matches.match_rationale: The full compliance monitoring cycle — detect accreditor standard updates, map changes to affected curricula, flag conflicts, route to program directors for review, and track resolution — is a multi-step workflow currently done manually and inconsistently. A task automation agent can orchestrate this end-to-end, ensuring no update to an accreditor standard falls through the cracks across the institution's multiple programs.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An accreditor change-monitoring agent that watches for published standards updates, automatically maps changes to relevant programs, generates a draft impact assessment, and creates review tasks for the responsible compliance officer — closing the loop from detection to resolution tracking.
